<!-- consumed by: .github/scripts/pretooluse-flutter-guard.sh -->
Never send `R` (hot restart) to the fifo in this setup — on a stale DWDS connection (common with the external Chrome extension), its timeout kills the `flutter run` process entirely and port 8090 goes dead. `r` (hot reload) is safe by comparison: it fails harmlessly on a stale connection, just without applying the change. If `r` isn't landing your change (structural changes like a new enum value, route-tree change, new top-level file, or a `const`/generic don't apply via hot reload at all), do a clean restart instead: `printf 'q' > /tmp/f8090`, then relaunch — see run-flutter-web-local's Clean restart section.
